sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application No. 157 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For order on office objection Nos. 11 & 27. 2. For order on CMA No. 968/25. (Exemption) 3. For order on CMA No. 969/25 (stay) 4. For hearing of main case. 24.11.2025 None present. On the last date, following order was passed:- “10.11.2025 Mr. Moiz Haideri, advocate for the applicant. 1. Urgent application is granted. 2-5. Prima-facie the order impugned is that of remand. Learned counsel remains unable to articulate any question of law arising there from. At his request adjourned to 24.11.2025.” Today, applicant remains unrepresented without intimation or justification despite a fixed date having been given. It appears that the applicant is not interested in proceeding with this matter, therefore, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, this reference is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Ayaz p.s.
